input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install the latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find the exact MAUI workload ID
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

The Windows (WinUI 3) target requires the Windows App SDK, which comes with the VS Build Tools.

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# From an elevated prompt — adjust path if using BuildTools
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Install Android SDK & JDK

MAUI's workload installs some Android pieces, but you may need to manage SDK platforms explicitly.

```powershell
# Check what the MAUI workload already installed
dotnet workload list

# The Android SDK is typically placed here after workload install:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"

# Discover installed & available Android platforms
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install a specific API level (e.g., 35 for Android 15)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

**JDK** — the MAUI workload bundles a compatible JDK. Verify:

```powershell
# Find the bundled JDK
Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" -Directory | Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1

# Or install explicitly
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17
```

## Step 5 — Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Set ANDROID_HOME (persist for current user)
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")

# Set JAVA_HOME to the bundled or installed JDK
$jdk = (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" -Directory |
  Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $jdk, "User")

# Refresh the current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME","User")
$env:JAVA_HOME    = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME","User")
```

## Step 6 — Create an Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
$sdkMgr = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"
$avdMgr = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# Install a system image
& $sdkMgr "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

# Create AVD
& $avdMgr create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_7"

# Launch
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## Step 7 — Enable Windows Developer Mode

Required for deploying WinUI 3 / MAUI Windows apps:

```powershell
# Elevated prompt
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## Step 8 — Create, Build & Run

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI 3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (must be running)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## Step 9 — Verify Everything

```powershell
# One-liner health check
dotnet workload list; dotnet --info
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Adjust `net9.0` to `net10.0` if you installed .NET 10 instead.
- The `--list` flag on `sdkmanager` is your best friend for discovering available API levels and build-tools versions dynamically.
- If Hyper-V is disabled, enable it for emulator acceleration: `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`.
